title: IACS UR E27 OT-equipment cyber-resilience attestation register — equipment-level evidence chain
exclusions: Excludes equipment installed before E27 effective date that is grandfathered under the vessel's existing class survey.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.59.16` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `iacs_clause="IACS-UR-E27-r3"`. Every row pins (imo_number, equipment_id, equipment_type, vendor, model, e27_revision, attestation_id, attestation_at, status).

compliance.assurance_rationale: IACS UR E27 Rev.3 (cyber resilience of on-board systems and equipment) applies at the equipment-level to all vessels in scope of UR E26. This UC anchors the equipment-level evidence.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that every E27-in-scope OT component carries a current attestation from its vendor (or a class-society type-approval), that the attestation matches the installed firmware/configuration, and that renewal is performed before expiry.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `IACS UR E27 Equipment Attestation Register` dashboard.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A propulsion-control unit installed under UR E26 scope shows no E27 attestation 14 days after commissioning. UC-22.59.16 MUST flag status=breach-no-attestation.
controlTest.negativeScenario: An OEM-provided E27 attestation is current and the installed firmware matches the attested revision. UC-22.59.16 MUST record status=compliant.

dataSources: KV Store `iacs_ur_e27_attestation_lookup` (equipment-level register). KV Store `imo_crar_components_lookup` (cyber-vulnerable component register, from UC-22.59.3). Cisco Cyber Vision / Claroty / Nozomi / Dragos firmware-discovery events (`cisco:cybervision:asset`, `claroty:ctd:asset`, `nozomi:guardian:asset`, `dragos:platform:asset`).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision (Splunkbase 6071), Splunk Add-on for Claroty (Splunkbase 5018), Splunk Add-on for Nozomi Networks (Splunkbase 4894), Splunk Add-on for Dragos (Splunkbase 5048), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup imo_crar_components_lookup
| where e27_in_scope = "true"
| lookup iacs_ur_e27_attestation_lookup imo_number equipment_id OUTPUT attestation_id e27_revision attestation_at attested_firmware
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval status = case(
    isnull(attestation_id), "breach-no-attestation",
    isnotnull(attested_firmware) AND installed_firmware != attested_firmware, "breach-firmware-mismatch",
    e27_revision != "Rev.3", "breach-revision-mismatch",
    1=1, "compliant")
| where status NOT IN ("compliant")
| sort 0 imo_number equipment_id
| table imo_number equipment_id equipment_type vendor model installed_firmware attested_firmware attestation_id e27_revision status
description: Tracks every E27-in-scope OT component against the vendor / class-society attestation register; raises an alert when no attestation exists, the installed firmware drifts from the attested version, or the attestation revision is outdated.
value: E27 is the equipment-level counterpart of E26. Firmware drift between the attested baseline and the installed equipment is the single most common audit finding; this UC closes the gap.
implementation: (1) Build the E27 attestation KV Store. (2) Wire the supply-chain / engineering department to register every OT component delivery against UR E27 scope. (3) Compare the OT-discovery feed against the attested firmware. (4) Schedule UC-22.59.16 daily; route P3 for drift findings.
visualization: (Panel 1) Fleet E27 equipment attestation table. (Panel 2) Firmware-drift count per vessel. (Panel 3) Top vendors by attestation gaps. (Panel 4) Attestation-revision mix.

references.title: IACS UR E27 — Cyber Resilience of Systems and Equipment, Rev.3 (2024)
IEC 61162-460 — Maritime navigation and radiocommunication equipment and systems — Digital interfaces

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Firmware-version aliasing**: the OEM ships v3.4.1 but the OT-discovery sensor reports v3.4.1-build42; align the comparison logic to ignore build-suffix differences. 2. **Replacement spare parts**: an emergency engine-control card swap may briefly create an attestation gap; honour a 14-day grace window before raising P3.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_16_exceptions` with the Company Security Officer documented approval.
reviewer: Maritime Cyber SME Panel + Company Security Officer + Marine Engineer
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.3, UC-22.59.7, and UC-22.59.15 in production.
- OT-discovery sensor coverage on every vessel.

## Step 1 — E27 attestation KV Store

```ini
[iacs_ur_e27_attestation_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.imo_number = string
field.equipment_id = string
field.attestation_id = string
field.e27_revision = string
field.attested_firmware = string
field.attestation_at = time
field.surveyor = string
```

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.16: IACS UR E27 Equipment Attestation Register]
cron_schedule = 0 5 * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.16,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IACS-UR-E27-r3
alert.severity = 3
```

## Step 3 — Maritime-specific notes

- **Type approval vs ship-specific attestation**: some vendors hold a class-society type approval that covers a model family; the register must map type-approval → equipment instance.
- **End-of-life firmware**: when a vendor declares EOL on an attested firmware, the company must plan an upgrade campaign; this UC flags the trigger event.
grandmaExplanation: Each critical piece of equipment on a ship — its engines, navigation, cargo controls — needs a cyber-safety certificate from the maker. We keep a list of every piece and its certificate, and we shout if any equipment is missing one or running software that doesn't match what was approved.

splunkbaseApps.name: Splunk Add-on for Cisco Cyber Vision
